title: VPN for Streaming 2026: Which Services Still Work and Which Are Blocked
description: Not every VPN unblocks Netflix, Disney Plus, or BBC iPlayer. This guide covers which VPNs consistently work for streaming in 2026 and what to check before buying.
content:
Streaming services have gotten much better at blocking VPNs since 2020. Netflix in particular runs one of the most aggressive IP detection systems in the industry. A VPN that worked six months ago may not work today. This guide focuses on what actually works in mid-2026 and what to look for.

The core problem is IP reputation. When a VPN provider registers a server IP address, Netflix and similar services check whether that IP belongs to a known data center or residential ISP. Data center IPs are flagged as VPN traffic and blocked. Residential IPs slip through because they look like normal home connections.

This is why expensive, well-resourced VPNs tend to work better for streaming than cheap ones. They invest more in rotating IP addresses and maintaining residential or obfuscated IP pools. ExpressVPN, NordVPN, and Surfshark have dedicated teams that respond when specific IPs get blocked. A /month service does not have this infrastructure.

For Netflix specifically: the library you access depends on where the VPN server is located. A US server gives you the US library, which is typically the largest. UK servers give access to different originals. Japanese Netflix has unique anime content. BBC iPlayer requires a UK IP and is one of the harder services to unblock because it also checks for UK billing address in some cases.

For Disney Plus: the situation is simpler. US servers work reliably with the VPNs that invest in streaming optimization. The international Disney Plus libraries are smaller than Netflix's regional variance, so most people are just trying to access US content from abroad.

Sports streaming (DAZN, ESPN Plus, Sky Sports) is the hardest category. These services update their blocklists frequently and sometimes block entire server ranges from specific providers. If sports streaming is your primary use case, test before committing to a yearly subscription.

The practical advice: use a VPN with a money-back guarantee. Test it against the specific service you care about within the refund window. If it does not work, get your money back and try another. Most reputable VPNs offer 30-day money-back guarantees.
